data.headline: Wait — before you go, your dog's itching might start somewhere you haven't looked
data.subheadline: If you came here for the paw licking, the gunky ears or the loose stools, you're in the right place. Keep scrolling.
data.trustLine: 20,000+ dogs helped in the last 12 months • 4,537+ verified reviews

data.headline: 5 Reasons Dog Owners Are Switching to Good For Pets 5 Strain Probiotic+
data.subheadline: The cold-filled daily sprinkle capsule that works on the gut — so the paw licking, itchy skin, gunky ears and loose stools settle from the inside out.
data.trustLine: Co-developed with practising UK vet Dr Kishan Vara MRCVS • 90-day money-back guarantee

data.heading: 1. Some owners see a difference in days
data.body:
Here's the honest version, because you've been promised quick fixes before and they didn't land. Skin and coat take time — usually eight to twelve weeks, because the gut is genuinely rebalancing rather than masking a symptom. But the gut is often the first thing to settle, and plenty of owners notice firmer stools and a calmer dog inside the first week or two. Julie started hers on the 24th of January and saw the paw licking stop. Elmo, an 8-year-old rescue, was firmer and more settled within four days. We won't pretend that's every dog — but it happens often enough that we want you to know it's possible.

What's actually going on is simple. 5 Strain Probiotic+ floods the gut with five research-backed strains, feeds them with a chicory-root prebiotic, and adds digestive enzymes to help break food down. The good bacteria establish, crowd out the unfriendly ones and support the gut lining — and because the gut and skin are linked, the things you see on the outside start to follow. It's a real reset, not a sticking plaster, which is why the early wins build into lasting ones if you stay consistent. And if it isn't right for your dog, the 90-day money-back guarantee takes the wait off your shoulders.

image.role: Happy golden retriever on grass beside the bottle

data.heading: 2. It works on more than one problem — because they often share one root
paragraphs: If your dog licks their paws raw in summer, scratches at flaky skin, has ears that turn gunky again no matter what you clean them with, scoots across the carpet or has stools you can never quite predict — you've probably treated each of those as a separate battle. Cream for the skin, drops for the ears, a diet change for the tummy, another supplement for the coat. Money out, brief relief, back to square one. It's exhausting, and it's easy to feel like you're failing a dog you love.

Here's the reframe that changes everything. A large share of a dog's immune system lives in and around the gut, and the gut and skin are linked — the gut-skin connection. When the microbiome is out of balance, the knock-on effects surface on the outside: itchy skin, paw licking, gunky ears, scooting, a dull coat, loose stools and wind. Owners spend months treating the symptom while the root sits untouched in the gut.

That's why one daily capsule can support so many of the things you're seeing at once. It isn't a different product for each problem — it's support for the system underneath them all. Five strains, a prebiotic to feed them, and enzymes to help digestion. Calm the inside and the outside follows.
It supports a healthy gut microbiome, firmer and more regular stools, the gut-skin connection for healthy skin and a glossy coat, natural immune defences and a healthy yeast balance.
One root. One daily capsule.

data.heading: 3. Safe and suitable for daily use, across breeds and sizes
data.body:
We get the worry — you've already tried a few things, and the last thing you want is to upset a sensitive dog further. So here's the plain version. 5 Strain Probiotic+ is a gentle daily formula with five research-backed strains, a chicory-root prebiotic and a digestive enzyme complex, in a cold-filled vegan capsule. There are no unhealthy fillers and no grains — nothing bulking it out or upsetting a delicate tummy. The full ingredient list is printed on the pack, so if your dog reacts to something, you can check it for yourself rather than take our word for it.

It's made in the UK to GMP standards and co-developed with practising UK vet Dr Kishan Vara MRCVS — real veterinary input in the formula, not marketing dressed up as expertise. The capsule shell is vegan HPMC, so it suits vegetarian and vegan households too.

It's suitable for daily, long-term use across breeds and sizes, dosed by weight — from a small terrier to a giant Mastiff. Nicola's old English Mastiff was on them three weeks before her ears, skin, paws and stools all turned a corner.

A quick word on who it isn't for: not puppies under 12 weeks, and not pregnant or lactating dogs. If your dog is under veterinary supervision or on medication, check with your vet first — sensible advice with any new addition. Probiotics are generally very well tolerated, and some dogs have slightly softer stools for the first week as the gut adjusts, which is exactly why you start on half a dose for five to seven days.

No refrigerating, no special diet, no fuss.

Just a steady, daily bit of support you can keep going for years.

image.role: German Shepherd on grass beside the bottle

data.heading: 4. No pilling, no fight — it's a sprinkle capsule
paragraphs: If your dog is fussy, or you dread the wrestling match every time you try to give a tablet, this is the bit that makes daily use actually doable. You never have to make them swallow anything. 5 Strain Probiotic+ is a sprinkle capsule — you simply twist it open and mix the contents straight into their food. The fine powder disappears into a bowl of kibble or a spoon of pate, and even the picky ones tend to get on with it.
It's not meant to be given whole. Twist, sprinkle, stir — done.

The dosing is foolproof too, because it goes by weight. Up to 25kg is one capsule a day; 25 to 40kg is two; over 40kg is three. Start on half the daily amount for the first five to seven days to ease the gut in gently, then move up to the full dose. Miss a day? No drama — just carry on the next day. There's nothing to measure out by hand and nothing to second-guess.

Elaine's fussy yellow Lab needs a bit of pate to take hers — "but it's worth it," she says. That's the honest reality with a fussy dog, and the sprinkle format is what makes it work. There's no chasing them round the kitchen, no spitting it back out, no half-eaten tablet under the sofa. Once it's mixed into a meal they love, it's just part of dinner — which is the only way a daily routine ever sticks for the long haul.

data.heading: 5. The risk sits with us, not you
data.body:
No honest brand can promise a result for every single dog — and we won't insult you by pretending otherwise. You've been let down before, and you're right to be wary. So instead of a big claim, here's a 90-day money-back guarantee. Give it a proper go, sprinkle it on daily, and if your dog isn't better for it, you get your money back. The wait, and the worry about wasting more money, is on us.

That's not a throwaway line. It's backed by 20,000+ dogs helped in the last 12 months and 4,537+ verified reviews — real dogs, named breeds, exact symptoms, plenty of them said by people as sceptical as you are now.

And there's one more thing we're proud of. We donate 51% of our profits to UK animal welfare charities including the RSPCA, Dogs Trust and Guide Dogs. A brand that gives away half its profit has nothing to hide — and when you help your dog, you help a dog in need. We'd rather show you the label and the guarantee than scare you into buying. That's the whole point.

image.role: Owner showing the pack to a senior Beagle by a sunny window

data.heading: Everything in one capsule — and why owners stick with it
paragraphs: So what's actually inside, and why does it work when the last few things you tried didn't?

Three jobs in one cold-filled capsule. First, the probiotics: five research-backed strains — Lactobacillus acidophilus, Lactobacillus rhamnosus, Lactobacillus brevis, Lactobacillus plantarum and Bifidobacterium lactis — at 5 billion live cultures (CFU) per serving, up to 20x the live cultures of some competitors. Second, the prebiotic: chicory-root inulin to feed those good bacteria so they establish and stay effective. Third, digestive enzymes to help break food down so nutrients are absorbed properly. Most rivals do one of these. This does all three.
Here's the part that explains the past failures.
Probiotics are live organisms, and live organisms are fragile.

Heat kills them. Moisture activates and exhausts them early. Most dog probiotic chews are baked and then sit in a moist tub — so the bacteria are often dead, or dying, before your dog ever eats them. A big number on a chew tub means nothing if it doesn't survive to the dog.
That's why ours is cold-filled.
Cold-filled in a sealed capsule, heat and moisture free, so the live cultures actually reach the gut alive. It's the difference between paying for a label and paying for something that works.
Katie switched from baked chews:
"Two and a half weeks on these and the difference is already massive."
Tanya did too:
"These are another level completely. What a difference they have made."
That's the cold-filled advantage in two owners' words.
Now the value, because £44.99 sounds like a lot until you do the maths.
It's up to 54% cheaper per serving than our top 10 competitors for a 20kg dog.
One tub is 90 sprinkle capsules. For a dog up to 25kg that's around 90 days.
And it's far cheaper than the alternative you already know about.
Chris put it plainly: the vet stuff was £140 for two weeks and nothing worked; this lasts about two months. Christine halved her trips to the vet.
That's the value story: more live cultures, cheaper per day, far cheaper than the vet.
No fillers. No grains. Human-grade ingredients.
Made in the UK to GMP standards.
Co-developed with practising UK vet Dr Kishan Vara MRCVS, who's on record: "Daily probiotics and enzymes make a meaningful difference to a dog's digestion."
He helped develop the formula — that's real veterinary input, not a stock photo and a slogan.
And then there are the owners. The ones who started sceptical and ended up telling everyone.
Sherry's Pomeranian lost his coat to a yeast issue and got it back. Gareth's Staffy went from flat and off-colour to "a healthy, happy, energetic dog."
Pug Rolo's owner had tried everything for his allergies. "I was sceptical that a probiotic could help, but after a few weeks his skin isn't itchy anymore, his coat looks amazing."
Joanne gave it a year. "After 12 to 14 weeks I noticed a huge change. She stopped chewing her paws and scratching her ears, and her ears are clean and healthy too."
These aren't polished testimonials. They're worn-down owners who finally found the thing that worked.
The throughline is always the same.
A dog that stops licking. Ears that clear. Stools that firm up. A coat that comes back. Energy that returns.
"It's like having a younger version of her back."
That's the transformation on offer — and the 90-day guarantee means the only risk in trying it is leaving things exactly as they are.
One capsule a day, sprinkled on dinner. That's all it takes to start.
20,000+ dogs have already started. Yours could be next.

items.q: How long until I see results?
items.a: It varies by dog. Some owners notice changes in the first week or two — often firmer stools first. Skin and coat usually build over 8 to 12 weeks as the microbiome rebalances. The 90-day money-back guarantee covers the wait.

items.q: Will it actually work for my dog?
items.a: No honest brand can promise a result for every dog — which is exactly why there's a 90-day money-back guarantee. What we can show you is 20,000+ dogs helped in the last 12 months and 4,537+ verified reviews across breeds, ages and the exact symptoms you're dealing with.

items.q: I've tried probiotics before and they did nothing. Why is this different?
items.a: Most are baked chews where the bacteria are largely dead on arrival, or token doses. This is cold-filled, heat and moisture free, with 5 billion live cultures per serving, plus a chicory-root prebiotic and digestive enzymes to help them establish. Different product, different odds.
Give it a proper go daily, and if your dog isn't better for it, you get your money back. The risk sits with us, not you.

items.q: Is it co-developed with a vet?
items.a: Yes. It was co-developed with practising UK vet Dr Kishan Vara MRCVS, who's on record: "Daily probiotics and enzymes make a meaningful difference to a dog's digestion."

items.q: Is it safe for my dog?
items.a: Probiotics are generally very well tolerated. It's a gentle daily formula with no unhealthy fillers or grains, in a vegan capsule, made in the UK to GMP standards. Some dogs have slightly softer stools for the first week as the gut adjusts, which is why you start on half a dose for 5 to 7 days.
Yes. Older dogs often benefit from daily digestive and microbiome support.

items.q: Is it suitable for all breeds and sizes?
items.a: Yes, from 12 weeks of age, dosed by weight — up to 25kg, 25 to 40kg, and 40kg+.

items.q: How do I give it? My dog is fussy.
items.a: It's a sprinkle capsule, not a tablet to swallow. Twist it open and mix the contents into food, once daily by weight. Start with half the daily dose for 5 to 7 days, then move to the full dose. Even fussy dogs tend to take it mixed into a meal — some owners use a little pate.

items.q: Is it chicken flavoured? Does it contain chicken?
items.a: Yes, it has a natural chicken flavouring, which is what makes it easy to sprinkle over food and gets even fussy dogs eating it. It's free from grains and unhealthy fillers. If your dog has a diagnosed chicken allergy or another known sensitivity, get in touch before starting and we'll help you decide.

items.q: How long does one tub last?
items.a: 90 capsules per tub. A dog up to 25kg (1 capsule a day) gets about 90 days; 25 to 40kg (2 a day) about 45 days; 40kg+ (3 a day) about 30 days.

Look at what it replaces, not what it costs. It's up to 54% cheaper per serving than our top 10 competitors for a 20kg dog, and far cheaper than repeat vet visits, steroids and ear drops. One tub lasts a smaller dog around 90 days. And the 90-day money-back guarantee makes trying it low risk.

items.q: Can I give it alongside medication?
items.a: Generally yes, but if your dog is on medication, antibiotics, is pregnant or lactating, or immunocompromised, check with your vet first.

items.q: Will it cause loose stools at first?
items.a: Some dogs have slightly softer stools in the first week or two as the gut adjusts. The half-dose start for 5 to 7 days helps ease them in.
No fridge needed. Store below 25°C in a cool, dry place, out of reach of children.
We give 51% of our profits to UK animal welfare charities including the RSPCA, Dogs Trust and Guide Dogs. Helping your dog helps dogs in need.
In the UK, to GMP standards. Human-grade ingredients, a full label you can read, and no mystery powder from an unnamed factory abroad.
